properties.name: Enbridge Mainline
properties.commodity: Crude oil — light, heavy, and diluted bitumen (WCS, synthetic crude)

to.name: Superior, WI / Sarnia, ON / US Gulf Coast

keyFacts: The largest crude oil transportation system in North America by capacity.
Multiple parallel lines (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6A, 6B, 9, 10) carry different grades to refineries in the US Midwest, eastern Canada, and the Gulf Coast.
Line 5 crosses the Straits of Mackinac in Michigan — politically contested for decades over environmental risk.
Line 3 was replaced in 2019–2021 — Enbridge's largest project ever (~$10B CAD).
Originally built in 1950 as the Interprovincial Pipe Line; rebranded as Enbridge in 1998.
properties.notes: On the map: only the western Canadian trunk (Edmonton → Hardisty → through the prairies to the Manitoba / US border) is rendered. The system continues into the US Lakehead network, then re-enters Canada at Sarnia, ON.

properties.name: Trans Mountain Pipeline System
properties.commodity: Crude oil — light crude and diluted bitumen; some refined products
keyFacts: The only Canadian pipeline carrying crude to a Pacific tidewater port — strategic Asian-market access.
TMX (Trans Mountain Expansion) entered service in June 2024, lifting capacity from ~300,000 to 890,000 bbl/day.
Owned by the Government of Canada since 2018, after Kinder Morgan exited amid permitting risk.
Crosses the Rockies via the Yellowhead corridor (Edmonton → Jasper → Kamloops → Hope → Burnaby).
TMX commissioning has narrowed the WCS / WTI price differential as Canadian crude reaches new buyers.
properties.notes: The original Trans Mountain Pipeline (TMP) entered service in 1953. The expansion (TMX) twins most of the corridor and tripled effective egress capacity in 2024.

properties.name: Keystone Pipeline (Phases 1–2)
properties.commodity: Crude oil — primarily diluted bitumen (WCS) and light synthetic crude
keyFacts: Connects Alberta's oil sands to US refining hubs at Cushing, OK ('the pipeline crossroads of the world') and the Texas Gulf Coast.
Border crossing at Haskett, MB / Walhalla, ND.
Distinct from the cancelled Keystone XL (2021) — the proposed shorter, larger-diameter extension across Montana and South Dakota.
TC Energy spun off its liquids business as 'South Bow' in October 2024 — Keystone is now a South Bow asset.
A December 2022 spill in Kansas (~14,000 bbl) led to capacity restrictions while remediation was underway.
properties.notes: On the map: only the Canadian segment (Hardisty, AB → border crossing at Haskett, MB) is rendered. The system continues through North Dakota, South Dakota, Nebraska, then south to Cushing, OK and the Texas Gulf Coast.
